title_en: The Fibre Fad Keeps On Moving

paragraphs.en: The Fibre Fad Keeps On Moving
How a nutritional trend brought bathroom talk into the realm of food culture.
paragraphs.zh: 一场营养趋势如何把“上厕所”话题带进了饮食文化。

paragraphs.en: You could think of cultivating your gut microbiome as “feeding your Eden,” according to Jeni Britton, who débuted a line of dehydrated-fruit bars last year.Photo illustration by Jason Fulford and Tamara Shopsin
paragraphs.zh: 你可以把培育肠道微生物群想象成“喂养你的伊甸园”，这是 Jeni Britton 的说法——她去年推出了一系列脱水水果棒。图片插画由 Jason Fulford 和 Tamara Shopsin 绘制。

paragraphs.en: For years, Ariel Pastore-Sebring, a thirty-one-year-old recipe developer and comedian in Portland, Oregon, has been keeping notes for a potential cookbook. One idea was a chapter of recipes inspired, in part, by her time running track at the University of Pittsburgh. “I was very conscious of what I was putting in my body,” she told me recently, especially when it came to finding the right balance of simple carbohydrates and fibre. “Runners are pretty big on clearing out before a race.” On social media, her posts skew aspirational, often showcasing the art of the dinner party: gingham tablecloths set with fresh flowers, kitschy cocktail glasses, and jewel-toned, vegetable-strewn platters. When she decided to experiment with high-fibre recipes on Instagram and TikTok, she opted for candor. In a video featuring roasted sweet potatoes with sautéed greens and dressed lentils, she spoke into a roll of toilet paper as if it were a microphone. “Welcome to Blow Outz,” she announced, in the self-consciously goofy tone of a game-show host, “a new series of recipes to knock a few things loose.”
paragraphs.zh: 在美国俄勒冈州波特兰，一位三十一岁的菜谱开发者兼喜剧演员 Ariel Pastore-Sebring 多年来一直在为一本潜在的菜谱书做笔记。她有一个想法，是写一个受自己在匹兹堡大学田径队跑道训练经历启发的食谱章节。“最近我特别在意自己摄入的东西，”她最近对我说，尤其是如何找到“简单碳水化合物”和“纤维”的合适平衡。“跑步运动员在赛前往往会很在意把肠道清空。”在社交媒体上，她发布的内容更偏向“向上激励”，经常展示晚宴美学：带格子花纹的桌布配上鲜花、复古鸡尾酒杯，以及色泽饱满、堆满蔬菜的拼盘。当她决定在 Instagram 和 TikTok 上尝试高纤维食谱时，她选择了直白表达。在一段用烤红薯、快炒绿叶菜和酱拌扁豆的短视频里，她把卷筒卫生纸当作麦克风对着它说话。“欢迎来到 Blow Outz，”她用一种故意搞怪、像游戏节目主持人的腔调宣布道，“这是一个新系列，专门做一些让你‘松一松’的菜谱。”

paragraphs.en: To Pastore-Sebring’s shock, the video went viral. She added more episodes—a quinoa-apple salad with walnuts, chopped kale, and radicchio; a spin on som tum, the Thai shredded-papaya salad; and the bluntly named Poop Smoothie (nicknamed the Poothie by some fans), made from spinach, chia seeds, oats, and peanut butter—and soon had seventy thousand new followers. She had stumbled into a trend known as “fibremaxxing.” “The craziest part is that people feel like they can share with me what’s going on with their bowel movements,” she said. “I’ve been getting D.M.s from people, like, telling me about their poops, or saying, ‘Hey, this really helped me.’ ”
paragraphs.zh: 令 PASTORE-SEBRING 惊讶的是，这支视频在网上疯传。她又增加了更多内容：石榴籽苹果沙拉（加入核桃、切碎的羽衣甘蓝和菊苣）、泰式木瓜丝沙拉 som tum 的改良版，以及被一些粉丝戏称为“Poothie”的“便便奶昔”，主要成分是菠菜、奇亚籽、燕麦和花生酱。很快，她就又吸引了七万名新粉丝。她不小心踩中了一个叫作“fibremaxxing（纤维狂热）”的潮流。“最疯狂的部分是人们居然愿意和我分享他们的排便情况，”她说，“我收到过很多私信，大家都在跟我讲自己的便便，或者说‘嘿，这真的很有帮助。’”

paragraphs.en: I first sensed that fibre could be the new protein—a macronutrient that captures the Zeitgeist—last year, when Jeni Britton, the founder of the upscale brand Jeni’s Splendid Ice Creams, débuted a line of dehydrated-fruit bars called Floura. Made largely from cores and rinds, the bars were marketed as environmentally conscientious, but they were also meant to address a phenomenon that Britton calls the “fibre gap.” Less than ten per cent of Americans consume the daily recommended amount of dietary fibre, also known as roughage—the parts of plants that scrub through the digestive tract instead of being absorbed. (A single Floura bar contains about half the daily suggested intake.) Around the same time, I went out with three friends, all mothers in their early forties, for a monthly dinner that we jokingly call Women’s Group. One of them announced, conspiratorially, that another friend’s holistic physician had a maxim for the premenopausal: six prunes a day.
paragraphs.zh: 我第一次感觉到“纤维”可能会成为新一代“蛋白质”——一种抓住时代风向的营养素——是在去年。那时，Jeni Britton（高端品牌 Jeni’s Splendid Ice Creams 的创始人）推出了一款名为 Floura 的脱水水果棒系列。该产品主要由果核和果皮制成，主打环保理念，但同样也是为了应对 Britton 所说的“纤维缺口”现象。美国不足十分之一的人每天摄入的膳食纤维（也叫粗纤维）达不到每天推荐量，而这部分纤维其实是植物中不能被人体吸收、会在消化道里起到清洁作用的部分。 （一根 Floura 棒大约含有每日建议摄入量的一半。）就在差不多同一时期，我和三位朋友去吃月度聚餐，我们开玩笑把这个聚会叫作“女性小组”。其中一位神秘地告诉我，另一位朋友的整体医疗医生给围绝经期女性的“真理”是：每天吃六颗梅子。

paragraphs.en: Compared with protein, which lends itself to narratives of growth—more grams, more gains—fibre is a bit of a hard sell, likelier to be espoused by women in sensible shoes than by fitness junkies on Twitch. But the benefits of eating a high-fibre diet are arguably more significant. Gastroenterologists and influencers alike are eager to explain that fibre affects health beyond “gut motility,” formerly known as shitting: it slows digestion, which can make you feel full for longer and help manage blood sugar and body weight, and has been shown to lower cholesterol and blood pressure. Fibre can also be a prebiotic, feeding the trillions of gastrointestinal bacteria that support our immune and nervous systems. If proteinmaxxing conjures the rapacious spirit of an HGTV makeover show, fibremaxxing is like a PBS special about restoring a Victorian home.
paragraphs.zh: 与蛋白质不同，后者很容易讲述“更重、增长更快”的叙事故事——“多摄入几克就能更强壮”——相比，纤维就不那么容易被“卖”出去，似乎更适合穿着体贴鞋的女性谈起，而不是那些在 Twitch 上的健身狂热者。可高纤维饮食的益处其实可能更为重要。胃肠科医生和网红都急于解释：纤维会影响比“肠道蠕动”（过去常用更直白的说法来指排便）更广泛的健康问题：它会减慢消化过程，让你更容易有饱腹感，从而有助于血糖和体重管理；也被证明能降低胆固醇和血压。纤维还可以充当益生元，给我们的肠道细菌提供养分，而这些细菌会支持免疫系统和神经系统。 如果说 proteinmaxxing 会让人联想到 HGTV 风格的“改造营”里那种贪婪的精气神，那么 fibremaxxing 更像是一档 PBS 的节目：它讲的是如何修复一座维多利亚风格的老宅。

paragraphs.en: Britton became a zealot for fibre during the pandemic, not long after she’d decided to step away from the day-to-day operations of her ice-cream business. Stuck at home, in Ohio, “I just didn’t know who I was at all, so I went to the forest,” she told me. “I was walking ten miles a day, wandering the earth like Demeter looking for Persephone.” She also began to eat fresh blueberries by the pint. At first, she wondered if she was overdoing it—wasn’t fruit high in sugar?—but after a few months she’d lost weight and felt better than she had in years, a transformation that also helped her realize that she wanted a divorce. After educating herself on nutrition, Britton concluded that it was the fibre, stupid—and that she should try harvesting it from the parts of fruit that an ice-cream company would typically discard.
paragraphs.zh: Britton 在疫情期间几乎成了纤维的狂热倡导者。就在她决定抽身处理日常冰淇淋业务之后不久。她人在俄亥俄，被迫居家，“我当时完全不知道自己是谁，所以我就去了森林，”她告诉我，“我每天散步十英里，像德墨忒耳（Demeter）寻找珀尔塞福涅那样在大地上漫步。” 她也开始按品脱买新鲜蓝莓。起初她还怀疑自己是不是吃太多了——水果不是含糖很高吗？——但几个月后她减了体重，感觉状态也比这些年都好，这种变化也让她意识到自己想要离婚。她后来认真学习营养学后得出结论：问题其实在于纤维，没错，就是纤维（真是傻到家了）——她应该从那些冰淇淋公司通常会丢弃的果实部分里提取纤维。

paragraphs.en: The bars, which come in flavors like mango cardamom and brambleberry lavender, are packaged in luxe, color-blocked matte wrappers. Each is a chewy, dark-brown puck, slightly tacky to the touch. Most are speckled with bits of roasted almond; all taste predominantly of indeterminate dried fruit. When I asked Britton how she planned to overcome fibre’s less palatable associations, she reached for dreamier imagery: you could think of cultivating your gut microbiome as “feeding your Eden,” she said. “I think people are going to start to think of their insides as, like, this wilderness place that I can go, and it’s where my imagination lives, and my peace and my safety.” Laughing, she added, “Versus the idea of, just, poop.”
paragraphs.zh: 这些能量棒有芒果豆蔻、刺莓薰衣草等口味，包装成时髦的撞色哑光纸盒。每一支都是一块咀嚼感十足的深褐色小饼，摸起来略带黏性。多数表面都散落着烤杏仁碎屑；总体来说，味道以难以下咽但又很明显的干果风味为主。我问布里顿（Britton）她如何让纤维摆脱“不太好闻、不太好想要”的联想时，她说得更梦幻了一点：你可以把养护肠道菌群想象成“喂养你的伊甸园”。“我觉得人们会开始把自己身体内部当成一片野性之地，那是我的想象、我的宁静和我的安全所在。”她笑着补充：“而不是那种‘只是拉粪’的想法。”

paragraphs.en: In the past quarter century, food culture has become ever more personal and confessional; perhaps the inevitable conclusion was that it would encompass, well, its inevitable conclusion. Like so many trends, this one was pioneered by gay men: one of the early luminaries of FibreTok was a sex educator and amateur chef named Alex Hall, who posts recipes on an account called The Bottom’s Digest. I admit to feeling some relief around the breaking of this taboo—after all, everyone poops, and we might as well do it better. In France, a healthy bowel movement might serve as evidence of aesthetic finesse: according to at least one Reddit thread, to say “j’ai fait un perfect” is to brag about a stool so clean that it requires little to no toilet paper.
paragraphs.zh: 在过去的25年里，食品文化变得更加个人化和“自我坦白”；也许这个趋势不可避免的结局，正是把这种内容也纳入其中。和许多潮流一样，这一波同样最早是由同性恋男性推动的：纤维文化（FibreTok）的早期代表之一是名叫亚历克斯·霍尔（Alex Hall）的性教育者兼业余厨师，他在名为 The Bottom’s Digest 的账号发布食谱。我承认，看到这个禁忌被打破让我有种释然——毕竟，人人都会排便，我们不妨把它做好一点。在法国，一次健康的排便甚至可能算是一种审美功夫：在至少一个 Reddit 讨论串里，法国人说“j’ai fait un perfect”在炫耀一种非常干净、几乎不需要擦太多厕纸的排泄。

paragraphs.en: As it turns out, the appeal of fibre cuts across marketing idioms, whether it’s Pastore-Sebring’s bawdy millennial relatability, Britton’s candy-colored promise of nirvana, or the biohacking grindset embodied by the thirty-six-year-old entrepreneur Alan Lin. In 2025, Lin, a self-described “fibre daddy,” released a product called Liquid Salad, a thin green purée of apple, cucumber, kale, and celery, enhanced with potent prebiotics such as chicory-root inulin and chia seeds. It comes in the sort of plastic pouch that is often used for baby food, and it tastes, to me, like a pleasantly vegetal applesauce with a powerfully unpleasant aftertaste of stevia. (A version sweetened only with fruit juice is forthcoming.)
paragraphs.zh: 事实证明，纤维的吸引力能越过各种营销话术，无论是帕斯托尔-塞布林（Pastore-Sebring）那种大胆又带有千禧一代共鸣的说法、布里顿那种糖果色的“极乐境”承诺，还是36岁创业者林昂（Alan Lin）所代表的生物黑客式“硬核磨炼”心态。2025年，林推出了一个名为 Liquid Salad 的产品：一种薄薄的绿色浓浆，由苹果、黄瓜、甘蓝和芹菜打成，加入了像菊苣根低聚果糖（菊粉）和奇亚籽这样效力很强的益生元。它装在常见的婴儿食品塑料小袋里；对我来说，味道像清新的植物苹果酱，但后味里有很强烈的甜叶菊苦涩感。（一个只用果汁增甜的版本正在推出中。）

paragraphs.en: Lin got the idea for Liquid Salad on a trip he took to Japan, in 2024. He realized that the traditional Japanese diet, which is heavy in fibre, was optimal for digestion, and he marvelled at the abundance of nutritional-supplement pouches he saw at convenience stores. Back home in Seal Beach, California, he set out to make a high-fibre diet “extremely convenient for lazy Americans.” He earned a following, in part, with an absurd and tangentially related gimmick: a series of videos in which he wolfs down boiled eggs and other sulfuric foods in order to generate maximally offensive farts. “Every influencer is in Lululemon, and so fucking boring,” he told me recently. “I’m, like, Can’t you make it entertaining and fun?”
paragraphs.zh: 林是在2024年一次日本之行中想到 Liquid Salad 的主意。他意识到，日本传统饮食纤维含量高，这对消化特别有利；而他也惊讶于便利店里大量的营养补剂小包装。他回到加州塞尔海滩后，开始着手做一种“对懒人美国人来说极其方便”的高纤维饮食方案。林还靠一个看似离谱但又有点相关的噱头吸引了不少关注：他拍过一系列视频，里面他狂吞煮鸡蛋和其他含硫食物，目的就是制造“味道最冲、最难闻”的屁。“每个网红都在穿 Lululemon，超无聊，”他最近对我说，“我说，不能把它做得有趣又好玩吗？”

paragraphs.en: We’d met, ostensibly for lunch, at the Crema Café, a Seal Beach bakery known for its sandwiches and pastries. But when it came time to order, Lin, who wore a slim pair of Saint Laurent sunglasses and a mesh tank top that showed off his biceps, asked for only an almond-milk latte, unsweetened. He tries to avoid carbs, he explained, focussing mostly on protein and on fibre—the best macro for unblocking, as it were, one’s potential. “I wake up so excited,” he told me. “I take a huge dump every morning, and then I crush the MacBook for thirteen hours every day. This is, like, the most exciting thing in the world to me.” ♦
paragraphs.zh: 我们表面上是去吃午饭，在以三明治和糕点闻名的加州海豹海滩（Seal Beach）面包店 Crema Café 见面的。可当该点餐时，戴着一副修身圣罗兰太阳镜、上身穿着网状背心把二头肌都露出来的林，只点了一杯无糖杏仁奶拿铁。他解释说自己尽量少吃碳水化合物，主要靠蛋白质和纤维——也就是那种能够“解锁”一个人潜能的最佳宏量营养素。“我醒来时特别兴奋，”他告诉我，“我每天早上都会拉一大坨，然后整整十三个小时对着 MacBook 挤压（工作）。对我来说，这几乎是世界上最令人兴奋的事情了。” ♦
